meta.prompt: Re-comprehend cortex at HEAD 7db2dce; produce refreshed digest, data.json, and DELTA.md

system.name: cortex
system.summary: A portable second-brain library (`@kybernesis/brain-*`) — 7 public + 1 private ESM-only TypeScript npm packages. Stores conversations/notes/files on a timeline, recalls by meaning (RRF-fused multi-channel search), and builds a knowledge graph of people, facts and relationships. Provider-pluggable: three seams (Storage, Embedding, LLM) wired at startup. Now also supports versioned schema migration, file provenance tracking, kernel-owned reconcile, and a 5-channel intent-gated retrieval arc. Powers KAD and KBDE; Cortex is now canonical (ADR-0015) and may move ahead of KAD.
system.monorepo: pnpm workspace, 7 public packages versioned in lockstep (current 0.12.0); brain-bench private at 0.1.0
system.governance: Cortex canonical (ADR-0015) — may improve freely, prove no-regression via brain-bench before/after, inform KAD/KBDE on version bump

seams.name: Storage
seams.powers: All persistence — timeline, entities, facts, sleep, vectors; plus migration runner and reconcile SQL primitives

seams.name: LLM
seams.powers: Relationship/fact extraction, contradiction detection, profiles, sleep reasoning

packages.name: @kybernesis/brain-core
packages.what: The kernel — timeline, entity-graph, facts, vectors, retrieval, sleep, and now reconcile. Holds all algorithms; has no built-in backends. Consumers wire the three provider seams at startup. New at HEAD: diffFilePaths (pure diff), reconcile() (applies repairs), effectiveConfidence/factDecay (read-time decay), parseRelationalQuery (intent parser), resolveSourceTitles wiring. ✅verified
key_files: src/index.ts — public API surface (flat async exports by subsystem)
src/entity-graph.ts — entity CRUD, mentions, typed relationships, profiles, insights, contradictions
src/fact-store.ts / fact-extractor.ts / fact-retrieval.ts / fact-contradiction.ts / fact-temporal.ts — fact lifecycle
src/fact-decay.ts — effectiveConfidence (read-time decay, useDecay flag) NEW
src/relational-intent.ts — parseRelationalQuery intent parser (pure regex, ReDoS-safe) NEW
src/reconcile.ts — diffFilePaths + reconcile() orchestrator (kernel owns policy) NEW
src/user-profile.ts — LLM-backed profile synthesis from facts

packages.name: @kybernesis/brain-contracts
packages.what: The seam definition package — Zod schemas, provider interfaces, shared constants. Two-plane StorageProvider (Repositories = CRUD; Domain = business-logic SQL). New at HEAD: reconcile types (DiskFileEntry, BrainFileEntry, FilePathsDiff); TimelineRepository reconcile primitives (listAllFilePaths, relabelFilePath, stampContentHash, removeFileCluster); SearchQueries.relationalCandidates; FactRetrievalQueries.resolveSourceTitles; HybridSearchOptions flags (useRelational, useLookup, useDecay); HybridSearchResult additions (rawSemanticDistance, rawKeywordScore, source_title). ✅verified
key_files: src/storage.ts — StorageProvider interface (all repos + domain planes; reconcile primitives added)
src/constants.ts — enums, SOURCE_CONFIDENCE, EMBEDDING_MODEL/DIM/INPUT_CAP, RRF_K=60, CLAUDE_MODEL_ALIASES
src/entity.ts / fact.ts / sleep.ts / timeline.ts — Zod row schemas
src/reconcile-diff.ts — DiskFileEntry / BrainFileEntry / FilePathsDiff types NEW
data_shapes: HybridSearchOptions: useRelational / useLookup / useDecay / expandQuery flags NEW

packages.name: @kybernesis/brain-storage-sqlite
packages.what: Default StorageProvider — better-sqlite3 backend. Now includes a full migration subsystem. timeline_events gained file_path + content_hash (migration 002). addEvent uses dual-key upsert on file_path so file rows converge. Implements all reconcile SQL primitives. sql-temporal.ts helper fixes expired-fact leak (julianday normalisation, 9 sites). ✅verified
key_files: src/provider.ts — StorageProvider factory; lazy-loads vector store; wires autoMigrate
src/index.ts — getDb() pooling + runMigrations + setAutoMigrate exports
src/timeline.ts — TimelineRepository (timeline_events with file_path/content_hash); dual-key upsert

packages.name: @kybernesis/brain-storage-vec
packages.what: VectorRepository seam — sqlite-vec backend for vector persistence and brute-force semantic search (vec_distance_l2). 1536-dim, fixed. Lazy-loaded; degrades to no-op if native binary can't load. Unchanged from 0.8.2. ✅verified
key_files: src/index.ts — getVectorDb()/closeVectorDb(), connection pool, schema DDL
data_shapes: chunk_meta (rowid, ts, source, content, origin_id) + chunk_meta_origin index

packages.name: @kybernesis/brain-embed-openai
packages.what: Tenant-aware EmbeddingProvider wrapping OpenAI text-embedding-3-small → 1536-dim vectors. Per-tenant API-key resolution with per-tenant client caching; degrades to null on missing key or failure. Unchanged from 0.8.2. ✅verified
key_files: src/index.ts — embed(t, text) + key resolution + client cache

packages.name: @kybernesis/brain-llm-claude
packages.what: LLMProvider invoking Claude via the local `claude` CLI as a subprocess. Never throws — resolves to null on spawn/exit/empty/timeout, with optional onError hook. 60s timeout (SIGTERM on hang). Unchanged from 0.8.2. ✅verified

packages.name: @kybernesis/brain-testkit
packages.what: In-memory fakes + conformance/parity harness — proves a provider implementation conforms to the contracts. Vitest-free conformance cases; runParityHarness compares baseline vs candidate by top-N overlap (default N=10, threshold 0.8). Unchanged from 0.8.2. ✅verified

packages.name: @kybernesis/brain-bench
packages.what: The no-regression gate. Diagnostic quality instrument asking 'is the brain actually good at being a brain?' — not contract conformance but memory quality. CLI bin `brain-bench`. Three suites: locomo (LoCoMo public corpus, source-hit@5 baseline ≈ 0.65), probes (6-faculty personal-probe suite against frozen live-brain copy), ingestion-fidelity. Faculties: recall, entity, temporal, multi_hop, currency, abstention (abstention measurable: false until KYB-81 calibrates threshold). Score = recall@k (partial credit) for source-hit faculties; currency = current source outranks superseded. Every retrieval arm change must show clean before/after on frozen snapshot before going default-on. Never writes to live brain, never commits personal corpus output. ✅verified
key_files: src/datasets/probes.ts — Probe type, FACULTIES array, loadProbes()
README.md — suites, usage, frozen-copy protocol, boundaries
data_shapes: Probe { probe_id, faculty, question, answer, expected_source_ids, superseded_source_ids?, answerable, excluded? }
FacultyScorecard { k, by_faculty: Record<Faculty, FacultyBreakdown>, query_time_model_calls, per_probe }

databases.name: timeline.db
databases.what: Timeline events and extracted facts, with FTS5 indexes. New at HEAD: file_path + content_hash columns on timeline_events (migration 002); idx_timeline_file_path index; _migrations ledger table (materialises on migration trigger). ✅verified

databases.name: entity-graph.db
databases.what: The knowledge graph: entities, mentions, typed relationships, merges, profiles, insights, contradictions. Migration baseline stamped (001-baseline, no DDL change). ✅verified

databases.name: sleep.db
databases.what: Sleep run lifecycle, maintenance queue, memory edges, telemetry, tier transitions. Migration baseline stamped. ✅verified

databases.name: vectors.db
databases.what: Embedding store owned by brain-storage-vec; only materialises when an embedding provider is wired. Migration baseline stamped. ✅verified

flows.name: INGEST — storeConversation (fire-and-forget)
steps: extractRelationships(t, fullText) — LLM identifies entities + typed relationships
filterNoiseEntities(...) — strip files/UUIDs/IPs/tech-terms/pronouns
addConversationToTimeline(...) — write timeline event; file rows use dual-key upsert on file_path (converges re-ingests to one row)
segmentText(...) → indexChunk(...) — embed each ~250-char segment to vectors
findOrCreateEntity / linkEntitiesWithType — write entity nodes + mentions + typed edges
extractFactsRealtime(...) — Haiku decomposes facts (category, confidence, expiry), capped 3/call
indexChunk(fullText) — single parent embedding when text is short (no segmentation)
flows.notes: Now persists file_path and content_hash on timeline events for file-type ingests

flows.name: RECALL — hybridSearch (RRF fusion, 5 channels)
steps: Channel 1: semanticSearch — embed query → nearest-neighbour vectors (always)
Channel 2: metadataCandidates — FTS5 MATCH + title/tag/summary scoring + tier/priority boosts (always)
Channel 3: temporalCandidates — regex date terms → timeline timestamp matches (fires on dates)
Channel 4: entityAugmentedCandidates (expandQuery) — REFUTED, default OFF; degrades every faculty

Channel 5: relationalCandidates — parseRelationalQuery intent parser (pure regex) → typed-edge graph walk; fires only on relationship/lookup query shapes; useRelational default ON (zero regression); useLookup sub-kind default OFF NEW
reciprocalRankFusion(lists, k=60) — sum 1/(k+rank+1) across channels per source_path
cap MAX_SEGMENTS_PER_PARENT=3 + enrichByPath (tier/priority multipliers)
filter (tier/priority/type/entity/date) + sort by hybridScore
optional haikuRerank + addRelatedMemories (sleep memory_edges, top 3)
surface rawSemanticDistance? and rawKeywordScore? pre-RRF on result NEW
flows.notes: The load-bearing principle: flat-score injection displaces recall. RRF fuses ranks not magnitudes; always-on arms crowd the right answer out of top-k. Arms must be intent-gated.

flows.name: FACT-FIRST RECALL — factFirstSearch (4-layer)
steps: Layer 1: FTS5 fact search + semantic fact search (fact:// vectors), merge by overlap
Layer 2: seed most-mentioned entities → traverseEntityGraph BFS 1-hop (hop penalty 0.7/0.5/0.3) → getFactsForEntity
Layer 2.5: scene expansion (nearby facts in same conversation, 0.6x) + bridge discovery (facts connecting >=2 top entities)
Layer 3: findSupportingSegments for parent conversation of each top fact
Layer 4: assembleContext to token budget + tie-break by source priority (bridge>direct>entity>graph>scene)
resolveSourceTitles(t, conversationIds) — cross-table join to recover human relpath as source_title NEW
Optional: effectiveConfidence decay (useDecay flag, default off) — weights all facts pre-sort NEW

flows.name: SLEEP — runSleepCycleNow (9-step, concurrency-guarded)
steps: 1 Decay — age-based priority decay + expire temporal facts + weekly confidence decay
2 Tag — AI-classify timeline events (topic/domain/priority)
3 Consolidate — merge near-duplicate events by normalized title (>=3 dupes; skip pinned)
4 Link — build memory_edges (related-memory graph) for recall enrichment
5 Tier — classify events hot/warm/archive
6 Summarize — AI summaries for events lacking them
7 Observe — detect recurring facts / contradictions / temporal trends
8 Profile — AI entity narrative profiles → entity_profiles
9 Entity-hygiene — orphan cleanup, pattern/noise deletion, same-name + variant-name merges, low-value prune, profile gen

flows.name: WATCHED-FOLDER INGESTION (daemon, KAD/KBDE)
steps: startWatchedFoldersScheduler — 60s sweep, per-agent throttle + mutex, skip remote agents
runSyncCycle Pass 1 — walkDir → mtime+size diff vs state: new→storeConversation(channel=watched-folder); changed→cleanup+re-ingest
Pass 2 — deleted files → cleanupFile + drop from state
Pass 3 — unwatched folders → cleanupFile all + drop state
saveState atomically to <home>/data/watched_folders_state.json (regenerable; not SQLite/cloud)
flows.notes: ⚠️ Lives in consuming daemon, not the library packages

flows.name: RECONCILE — reconcile() (kernel-owned, host-supplied diskList)
steps: HOST walks its watched roots, filters, and sha256-hashes each file → passes diskList: DiskFileEntry[]
diffFilePaths(diskList, brainList) — pure, zero I/O; validates entries; classifies: missing / stale / orphaned / renamed / baseline
renamed resolution: relabelFilePath(t, id, newFilePath) — relocates file_path only; cluster+enrichment+embeddings survive; no re-ingest
baseline stamp: stampContentHash(t, id, hash) — lazy legacy backfill for NULL-hash rows
orphan removal (removeOrphaned): removeFileCluster(t, filePath) — REFUSES if diskList empty while brain has file rows
missing + stale repair: calls opts.reingest callback (storeConversation) — LLM + embed wired by consumer
errors are isolated per item; report always returned; reconcile is idempotent (rerun to converge)
flows.notes: THE BOUNDARY: the brain (kernel) never touches the filesystem. Policy lives in kernel; SQL lives in backend. A future libsql/Turso backend inherits reconcile by implementing 4 SQL primitives.

channels.name: temporal
channels.fires: only on date terms in query

channels.name: relational
channels.fires: only when query parses as relational/lookup shape

retrieval.key_principle: Flat-score injection displaces recall. RRF fuses ranks not magnitudes; arms emitting flat-scored candidates crowd the right answer out of top-k. Arms must be intent-gated.

flags.arm: Channel 5 lookup sub-kind (most prone to displacement)
read-time fact decay (factFirst path only, corpus-dependent)
Haiku LLM rerank (only query-time LLM call)

adrs.title: Parity harness role (swap-day smoke test under ADR-0015)
Retrieval augmentation arms are intent-gated and opt-in, never always-on
adrs.summary: Arms that inject flat-scored candidates displace recall via RRF. Three measured results (always-on entity arm, expandQuery, lookup) all showed the same mechanism. Rule: an arm contributes only when the query asks for what the arm provides; arms that can displace recall are opt-in flags.

adrs.title: Read-time fact decay is opt-in and corpus-dependent
adrs.summary: Decay A/B on 8 brains: decay on halved overall performance (25%→11%). Mechanism: in a knowledge corpus, fact timestamp = ingest/write time, not assertion recency; age multiplier crushes score differentiation. Stays off by default (useDecay). 2026-06-14 Ian reframe: correct signal is use-recency not assertion-age; decay must be recoverable. Target: access-based decay (KYB-85).

adrs.title: Cortex is canonical and may move forward (supersedes ADR-0003 port-faithful rule)
adrs.summary: The port-faithful era is over: KAD/KBDE depend on the library. Cortex can improve freely; must not regress (brain-bench gate); starts from KAD shapes; informs consumers on version bump. Enables intent-gated arms, decay, expiry fixes. Drops behavioural byte-parity requirement.
